_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet-thinking)**_

Here's a PowerShell command to search your entire PC for that UUID:

```powershell
Get-ChildItem -Path C:\ -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-String -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List | Select-Object Path, LineNumber
```

**More targeted and faster options:**

Search just your Cursor folders:
```powershell
Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Users\New User\.cursor" -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-String -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List | Select-Object Path, LineNumber
```

Search all your coding projects:
```powershell
Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\coding" -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-String -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List | Select-Object Path, LineNumber
```

Search just agent transcript folders across all projects:
```powershell

```


**2. Search Cursor folders (with progress):**
```powershell

```

**3. Search all coding projects (with progress):**
```powershell
$files = Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\coding" -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; $total = $files.Count; $i = 0; $files | ForEach-Object { $i++; Write-Progress -Activity "Searching coding projects" -Status "Processing $($_.FullName)" -PercentComplete (($i / $total) * 100); $_ } | Select-String -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List | Select-Object Path, LineNumber
```

**4. Search agent transcript folders (with progress):**

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet-thinking)**_

You're right - the progress bar updates too fast. Here are better versions that show progress during file enumeration AND give more visible feedback:

**1. Search entire PC (better progress):**
```powershell
$files = @(); Write-Host "Enumerating files..."; Get-ChildItem -Path C:\ -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $files += $_; if ($files.Count % 1000 -eq 0) { Write-Host "Found $($files.Count) files so far..." } }; Write-Host "Searching $($files.Count) files..."; $i = 0; $files | ForEach-Object { $i++; if ($i % 100 -eq 0) { Write-Progress -Activity "Searching files" -Status "$i of $($files.Count) - Current: $($_.Name)" -PercentComplete (($i / $files.Count) * 100) }; $_ } | Select-String -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List | Select-Object Path, LineNumber
```

**2. Search Cursor folders (better progress):**
```powershell
$files = @(); Write-Host "Enumerating Cursor files..."; Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Users\New User\.cursor" -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $files += $_; if ($files.Count % 100 -eq 0) { Write-Host "Found $($files.Count) files so far..." } }; Write-Host "Searching $($files.Count) files..."; $i = 0; $files | ForEach-Object { $i++; if ($i % 50 -eq 0) { Write-Progress -Activity "Searching Cursor files" -Status "$i of $($files.Count) - Current: $($_.Name)" -PercentComplete (($i / $files.Count) * 100) }; $_ } | Select-String -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List | Select-Object Path, LineNumber
```

**3. Search coding projects (better progress):**
```powershell
$files = @(); Write-Host "Enumerating coding project files..."; Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\coding" -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $files += $_; if ($files.Count % 500 -eq 0) { Write-Host "Found $($files.Count) files so far..." } }; Write-Host "Searching $($files.Count) files..."; $i = 0; $files | ForEach-Object { $i++; if ($i % 100 -eq 0) { Write-Progress -Activity "Searching coding projects" -Status "$i of $($files.Count) - Current: $($_.Name)" -PercentComplete (($i / $files.Count) * 100) }; $_ } | Select-String -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List | Select-Object Path, LineNumber
```

**4. Search agent transcripts (better progress):**
```powershell
$files = @(); Write-Host "Enumerating transcript files..."; Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Users\New User\.cursor\projects" -Recurse -Include "*.jsonl" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $files += $_; Write-Host "Found $($files.Count) transcript files so far..." }; Write-Host "Searching $($files.Count) files..."; $i = 0; $files | ForEach-Object { $i++; Write-Progress -Activity "Searching agent transcripts" -Status "$i of $($files.Count) - Current: $($_.Name)" -PercentComplete (($i / $files.Count) * 100); $_ } | Select-String -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List | Select-Object Path
```

These will:
- Show "Enumerating files..." messages as files are being found
- Show periodic counts during enumeration (every 100/500/1000 files depending on expected volume)
- Show progress bar during the search phase that updates frequently enough to be visible
- Display the current file name being searched

_**Agent (claude-4.5-sonnet-thinking)**_

I see the issue! The results ARE being found, but they only display AFTER the entire search completes. You're canceling before it finishes. 

Here's a version that shows results **immediately as they're found**:

**1. Search entire PC (shows results immediately):**
```powershell
$files = @(Get-ChildItem -Path C:\ -Recurse -File -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -gt (Get-Date).AddDays(-5) }); Write-Host "Total files (last 5 days): $($files.Count)"; Write-Host "Searching...`n"; $i = 0; $files | ForEach-Object { $i++; Write-Host "`rSearched: $i / $($files.Count) ($([math]::Round(($i/$files.Count)*100,1))%)                    " -NoNewline; $match = Select-String -Path $_.FullName -Pattern "27b9768b-a600-4f34-b1bb-e08bd22702ef" -List -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; if ($match) { Write-Host ""; Write-Host "$($match.Path)     $($match.LineNumber)" -ForegroundColor Green } }; Write-Host ""
```

**2. Search Cursor folders (shows results immediately):**
```powershell

```
